Official News

Ambassador of Egypt to Uzbekistan visits TSMU Chirchik Branch

The visit strengthened academic dialogue, international relations and future cooperation in medical education between Tashkent State Medical University Chirchik Branch and representatives of Egypt.

Read ArticleView GalleryInternational Relations
Full Story

A meaningful visit for international cooperation.

Tashkent State Medical University Chirchik Branch hosted a distinguished visit from the Embassy of the Arab Republic of Egypt in Uzbekistan, creating an important opportunity to discuss academic cooperation, student support and future international relations.

The meeting brought together university leadership, faculty representatives, students and members of the visiting delegation. Discussions focused on the development of cooperation in medical education, strengthening cultural and academic ties, and supporting international students who choose Uzbekistan as a destination for higher medical education.

During the visit, participants exchanged views on academic partnerships, clinical education, research cooperation, student mobility and the role of international relations in preparing globally minded healthcare professionals. The event reflects the branch’s commitment to building a modern, open and internationally connected medical university environment.

International cooperation is an essential part of preparing future healthcare professionals who can understand global health challenges and serve diverse communities.

The visit also highlighted the growing importance of TSMU Chirchik Branch as a destination for international students and as a platform for academic dialogue between Uzbekistan and partner countries. Meetings of this kind support the university’s wider strategy to expand cooperation with embassies, universities, hospitals and educational organizations around the world.

Students and faculty members had the opportunity to observe international academic diplomacy in practice and to understand how global partnerships contribute to education, research, clinical training and professional development.
